pi_items.source: Mathematical verification: 47 kΩ = 47 × 1000 Ω = 47000 Ω. Confirmed in task description.
Mathematical verification: 100 μF = 100 × 10^-6 F = 0.0001 F. Confirmed in task description.
Mathematical calculation using τ = R × C formula. τ = 47000 Ω × 0.0001 F = 4.7 seconds. This is mathematically correct.
Tolerance specification from task.json. Range would be [4.653s, 4.747s]. Reasonable for engineering calculation.

pi_items.metadata_value: Implied as true by task requirements
pi_items.source: APK analysis of com.hsn.electricalcalculations app. Extracted all calculator names from classes.dex. NO 'RC Time Constant' calculator found. Available calculators include: 3 Phase Power calculations, Cable Size Calculator, Capacitive/Inductive Reactance, Ohm's Law variants, Resonant Frequency, Resistor/Capacitor/Inductor combinations, and many others, but NOT RC Time Constant. The app does contain Resonant Frequency Calculation and Capacitive/Inductive Reactance calculations, but NOT a dedicated RC Time Constant calculator.

privileged_info_summary: The RC circuit parameters and calculation are mathematically correct: τ = R × C where R = 47000 Ω, C = 0.0001 F, yields τ = 4.7 seconds with 1% tolerance range [4.653s, 4.747s]. However, analysis of the Electrical Calculations app (com.hsn.electricalcalculations) APK reveals it does NOT contain an RC Time Constant calculator. The app includes 60+ electrical engineering calculations but lacks this specific feature.
